name: Pinecone Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt butter over low heat; remove from heat.
2. Add cocoa and blend well.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine sugar, eggs, and vanilla; blend in the cocoa mixture.
4. Stir together flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda; add to cocoa-sugar mixture, beating until smooth.
5. Refrigerate dough about 1 hour or until firm enough to roll.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F.
7. Roll out a small portion of dough between two pieces of waxed paper to 1/8 inch thickness.
8. Cut into pinecone shapes using 2-inch or 2-1/2-inch oval cookie cutters.
9. Place cookies on a lightly greased baking sheet; lightly brush with corn syrup.
10. Arrange almonds in a pinecone fashion and lightly drizzle or brush with corn syrup.
11. Repeat with remaining dough.
12. Bake for 7-8 minutes, or until set.
13. Cool slightly, then remove from the baking sheet to a wire rack.
14. Allow cookies to cool completely.
Makes about 4 dozen cookies
